name: ctsearch
description: Recursively find files by name, type, size, and content from a chosen root, replacing find|xargs|grep pipelines. An entry matches only when all supplied predicates hold. Exit status is 0 if anything matched, 1 if not, 2 on error. Pattern arguments use substring->glob->regex promotion: text with no metacharacters is a literal substring; glob metacharacters (* ? [ ]) that are not a valid regex are treated as a glob; otherwise the pattern is used as a regex.

base.description: Search root, relative or absolute, independent of the current working directory.

name.description: File-name pattern. '|'-separated alternatives, each substring->glob->regex promoted and anchored to the whole name (e.g. '*.java|*.kt').

type.description: Restrict to entry kinds: f=regular file, d=directory, l=symlink. May be repeated or comma-joined.

grep.description: Content pattern, substring->glob->regex promoted and searched unanchored against file contents. Implies regular files.

size.description: Size predicate [+|-]N[k|m|g]: +N larger than, -N smaller than, N at least N. Applies to regular files.

hidden.description: Include dot-entries (names starting with '.'). Default: skipped.

list.description: Output mode: print one matching path per line. This is the default mode.

summary.description: Output mode: print counts only. Mutually exclusive with the other output modes.

detail.description: Output mode: print matches plus, for --grep, each hit as path:line:text. Mutually exclusive with the other output modes.

quiet.description: Output mode: print nothing; report via exit status only. Mutually exclusive with the other output modes.
